questions.text: Plane figures like squares and circles are ______ figures.
questions.explanation: They have only two dimensions: length and breadth.

questions.text: Solid shapes like cubes and spheres are ______ figures.
questions.explanation: They have three dimensions: length, breadth, and height/depth.

questions.text: The corners of a solid shape are called its:
questions.explanation: A vertex is a point where three or more edges meet.

questions.text: The line segments forming the skeleton of a solid shape are its:
questions.explanation: An edge is where two faces meet.

questions.text: The flat surfaces of a solid shape are its:
questions.explanation: Faces are the 2D shapes that make up the boundary of a solid.

questions.text: How many faces does a cube have?
questions.explanation: A cube is like a die; it has 6 square faces.

questions.text: How many vertices does a cube have?
questions.explanation: A cube has 4 top corners and 4 bottom corners.

questions.text: How many edges does a cube have?
questions.explanation: 4 top edges, 4 bottom edges, and 4 vertical edges.

questions.text: A skeleton-outline of a 3D shape that can be folded to make the shape is a:
questions.explanation: A net is a 2D pattern for a 3D object.

questions.text: Euler's Formula for polyhedrons is:
questions.explanation: Where F=Faces, V=Vertices, E=Edges. Applies to all simple polyhedrons.

questions.text: A triangular pyramid is also known as a:
questions.explanation: It has 4 faces, all of which are triangles.

questions.text: How many faces does a triangular pyramid have?
questions.explanation: 1 base and 3 lateral faces.

questions.text: A square pyramid has how many vertices?
questions.explanation: 4 on the base and 1 apex.

questions.text: A cylinder has ______ curved surface and ______ flat circular faces.
questions.explanation: Top and bottom are circular; middle is curved.

questions.text: Does a sphere have any vertices?
questions.explanation: A sphere is completely curved with no corners.

questions.text: Drawings on paper that create an illusion of 3D are called:
questions.explanation: Used to represent 3D solids on a 2D surface.

questions.text: In an isometric sketch, the measurements are ______ to the actual solid.
questions.explanation: Isometric dot paper helps maintain proportions.

questions.text: How many vertices does a cone have?
questions.explanation: A cone has one vertex (the apex).

questions.text: The cross-section of a cylinder when cut vertically is a:
questions.explanation: Imagine cutting a pipe along its length.

questions.text: The cross-section of a cylinder when cut horizontally (parallel to base) is a:
questions.explanation: The cut follows the circular base.

questions.text: A cube of side 2 cm has a volume (conceptual context):
questions.explanation: Volume = side^3 = 2 * 2 * 2 = 8.

questions.text: If F=5 and V=6, how many edges does the polyhedron have?
Which solid has 5 faces, 8 edges and 5 vertices?
questions.explanation: Matches the properties of a pyramid with a square base.

questions.text: A triangular prism has how many faces?
questions.explanation: 2 triangular bases and 3 rectangular lateral faces.

questions.text: How many vertices does a triangular prism have?
questions.explanation: 3 corners on each of the two triangular bases.

questions.text: Number of edges in a triangular prism is:
questions.explanation: 3 on each triangle and 3 connecting them.

questions.text: Shadow of a sphere under a overhead lamp is a:
questions.explanation: Spheres project circular shadows.

questions.text: Shadow of a cube when light is from side might be a:
questions.explanation: Depends on the angle of projection.

questions.text: Nets of a cube consists of ______ equal squares.
questions.explanation: Cube has 6 faces.

questions.text: Can a net of 4 triangles and 1 square form a square pyramid?
questions.explanation: Matches the surface count of a square pyramid.

questions.text: Top view of a glass (tumbler) is a:
questions.explanation: Drinking glasses have circular openings.

questions.text: Side view of a cylinder is a:
questions.explanation: Projects as a rectangle from the side.

questions.text: Which of these solids is not a polyhedron?
questions.explanation: Polyhedrons must have flat faces made of polygons (no curved surfaces).

questions.text: A die is a cube where sum of opposite faces is always:
questions.explanation: Standard property of a dice.

questions.text: Number of faces in a tetrahedron is:
questions.explanation: Tetra- means 4.

questions.text: Shadow of a book (rectangular solid) under light is a:
questions.explanation: Projection of its flat face.

questions.text: Section of a cone cut parallel to base is a:
questions.explanation: Follows the shape of the circular base.

questions.text: Polyhedron with least number of faces is:
questions.explanation: Minimum 4 faces are needed to enclose a solid.

questions.text: Oblique sketches focus on representing:
questions.explanation: Lengths are often not to scale in oblique sketches.

questions.text: Visualisation helps in understanding how 3D shapes look from ______.
questions.explanation: Solid geometry involves multiple viewpoints.
